title: Tellers
description: Receive and pay out money. Keep records of money and negotiable instruments involved in a financial institution's various transactions.

tasks.text: Balance currency, coin, and checks in cash drawers at ends of shifts and calculate daily transactions, using computers, calculators, or adding machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Receive checks and cash for deposit, verify amounts, and check accuracy of deposit slips.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor bank vaults to ensure cash balances are correct.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Cash checks and pay out money after verifying that signatures are correct, that written and numerical amounts agree, and that accounts have sufficient funds.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Count currency, coins, and checks received, by hand or using currency-counting machine, to prepare them for deposit or shipment to branch banks or the Federal Reserve Bank.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Enter customers' transactions into computers to record transactions and issue computer-generated receipts.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Examine checks for endorsements and to verify other information, such as dates, bank names, identification of the persons receiving payments, and the legality of the documents.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Resolve problems or discrepancies concerning customers' accounts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare and verify cashier's checks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Process transactions, such as term deposits, retirement savings plan contributions, automated teller transactions, night deposits, and mail deposits.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Answer telephones and assist customers with their questions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify transaction mistakes when debits and credits do not balance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Carry out special services for customers, such as ordering bank cards and checks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Sort and file deposit slips and checks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Receive and count daily inventories of cash, drafts, and travelers' checks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Order a supply of cash to meet daily needs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Arrange monies received in cash boxes and coin dispensers according to denomination.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Receive mortgage, loan, or public utility bill payments, verifying payment dates and amounts due.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Explain, promote, or sell products or services, such as travelers' checks, savings bonds, money orders, and cashier's checks, using computerized information about customers to tailor recommendations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Count, verify, and post armored car deposits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Obtain and process information required for the provision of services, such as opening accounts, savings plans, and purchasing bonds.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform clerical tasks, such as typing, filing, and microfilm photography.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compute financial fees, interest, and service charges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compose, type, and mail customer statements and other correspondence related to issues such as discrepancies and outstanding unpaid items.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Process and maintain records of customer loans.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Quote unit exchange rates, following daily international rate sheets or computer displays.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Issue checks to bond owners in settlement of transactions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inform customers about foreign currency regulations and compute transaction fees for currency exchanges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
